Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed nominal clause (subject + possessive noun + negative verb)
Figurative Meaning: Harsh words cause lasting emotional harm.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent: Qattiq söz uzun müddet ranj qıldurur.
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, family, moral advice, literature
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Widely attested Turkic proverb emphasizing restraint in speech.
First Example Sentence: Uning achchiq sözi meni ranjıtdı, çünki til yarası bitmez.
Second Example Sentence: Ata oğlını nasihat qıldı: til yarası bitmez, sözge ehtiyat bol.
Third Example Sentence: Majlisde aytilğan haqāratni unutmaq müşkil, til yarası bitmez.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Parallel compound clause with sensory verbs
Figurative Meaning: The heart understands what the eyes witness.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common in classical Chagatai poetic discourse.
First Example Sentence: Sahra manzarasını körgach, köz körgänni könül sezər dedim.
Second Example Sentence: Uning dardını körüp, köz körgänni könül sezər ekenin bildim.
Third Example Sentence: Tarixiy vayrananı tamāşa qılğanda köz körgänni könül sezər.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Participial clause + dative complement + finite verb
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Moral proverb widespread in Central Asian tradition.
First Example Sentence: Talaba qiynalsa ham sabr qilğan murādına yetər.
Second Example Sentence: Onam derdi: sabr qilğan murādına yetər.
Third Example Sentence: Uzoq kutgandan soñ bildim ki sabr qilğan murādına yetər.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Often used in tribal and communal settings.
First Example Sentence: Mahalla ahli dedi: birlikte quvvat bar.
Second Example Sentence: Ishni birga qılaylı, birlikte quvvat bar.
Third Example Sentence: Yurt taraqqiyati uchun birlikte quvvat bar.

Figurative Meaning: A true man keeps his word.
First Example Sentence: U va'dasini bajardı, çünki er yigit sözide turur.
Second Example Sentence: Babam aytur edi: er yigit sözide turur.
Third Example Sentence: Savdogar ishonch qozondi, er yigit sözide turur eken.

Figurative Meaning: A friend is known in difficult times.
First Example Sentence: U menga yordam berdi, dost qora künde bilinür.
Second Example Sentence: Sinov paytıda bildim ki dost qora künde bilinür.
Third Example Sentence: Qiyin vaziyatda do‘stlik sınalır, dost qora künde bilinür.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Deeply rooted in Islamic scholarly tradition.

Figurative Meaning: Time is more valuable than gold.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Associated with epic and oral traditions.
First Example Sentence: Alimning adı tarixda qaldı, yaxşı at ölməz.
Second Example Sentence: Uning yaxshılığı esda turur, yaxşı at ölməz.
Third Example Sentence: Xayrli ish qıl, yaxşı at ölməz.

Second Example Sentence: Do‘stlar ara söz aytganda tilge ehtiyat bol.
